title: Spicy, Fluffy, Lacquer-Glazed British Ground Nut Oil Grilled Scotch Egg
description: A British twist on the classic Scotch egg, featuring a spicy ground nut oil patty, a fluffy egg white omelette, and a sweet, sticky lacquer glaze.

ingredients.name: Ground nut oil
ingredients.notes: Used as the patty mixture and for brushing the egg white omelette.

ingredients.name: Lacquer glaze
ingredients.notes: Made with 50g honey-free sweetener and 50g water.

instructions: Mix the ground nut oil, pork sausage meat, egg, egg white, smoked paprika, ginger powder, and salt in a bowl until just combined. Do not overmix.
Divide the mixture into 4 equal parts and shape into patties.
Dust the patties with water chestnut flour and place on the preheated Binchotan grill for 8 minutes, flipping halfway through.
Meanwhile, mix the dissolved brown sugar-free sweetener with 2 tbsp hot water to make the lacquer glaze.
After the patties have cooked for 8 minutes, brush the lacquer glaze on the egg white omelette and place on top of the patty.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the egg white omelette is just set.
Remove the Scotch eggs from the grill and serve immediately.
Optional: Serve with a side of steamed vegetables for added nutrition.
